title: The Cuckoo's Egg
author: Cliff Stoll
summary: A real-world story of tracking an intruder through network logs, debugging system behavior, and learning how complex systems fail.
verdict: This book showed me that engineering is as much about curiosity and persistence as it is about technical skill.

details.whyRead: I wanted to understand how real systems are investigated when something breaks in the wild.
details.expectation: I expected an obscure history of early computing security incidents.
details.whatFound: Instead, I found a gripping narrative that connected system monitoring, human behavior, and the need to trace symptoms back to causes.
keyTakeaways: Good debugging is often slow, methodical, and based on evidence.
Complex systems hide their failures in small, subtle anomalies.
Investigation skills are as important as coding skills in system engineering.
details.whatChanged: It made me care more about observability, log analysis, and the process of following a failure from symptom to root cause.
details.favoriteQuote: The only thing more expensive than education is ignorance.
details.recommendation: Recommended for engineers and students who want a practical view of how systems are defended and diagnosed.
